title: 3 things the Bruins must fix if they want to punch their ticket to the playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' power play has declined post-Olympic break, ranking 28th in the NHL with a 12.8% success rate.
Morgan Geekie and Elias Lindholm have not scored at 5-on-5 play in the last 11 games, contributing to the team's offensive struggles.
Andrew Peeke's defensive pairing issues persist, with the Bruins being outscored in key matchups since returning from the Olympics.

title: 5 Red Sox takeaways as Wilyer Abreu and Eduardo Rodriguez lift Venezuela to World Baseball Classic title
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Wilyer Abreu hit a crucial home run in Venezuela's 3-2 victory over the USA in the World Baseball Classic final.
Former Red Sox pitcher Eduardo Rodriguez threw 4.1 scoreless innings, helping Venezuela secure its first tournament title.
Red Sox players Jarren Duran and Masataka Yoshida excelled with Mexico and Japan respectively, showcasing strong performances.

title: This Ashland mom is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children with complex medical needs and their parents
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mengyuan Flanagan from Ashland is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Michael Lisnow Respite Center.
The Michael Lisnow Respite Center supports children with complex medical conditions and provides relief for their families.
Flanagan started running three years ago, inspired by the determination of past Boston Marathon participants.

title: Western Mass. couple found guilty of manslaughter in death of foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker were found guilty of involuntary manslaughter in the death of their 10-month-old foster child.
The foster child died from treatable conditions, including strep infection and bronchopneumonia, which the couple failed to seek treatment for.
The sentencing for the couple is scheduled for March 19; they face up to 20 years for manslaughter and 2.5 years for child endangerment.

title: Major water main break in Jackson Square floods streets, causes detours
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A major water main break in Jackson Square flooded streets in Jamaica Plain Tuesday afternoon, causing traffic reroutes.
The 36-inch transmission main break affected areas beyond Egleston Square, with crews working into the evening to mitigate damage.
MBTA bus routes 14, 22, 29, 41, and 44 were detoured, while the Orange Line remained unaffected.

title: Why Jaylen Waddle’s trade to Broncos is bad news for the Patriots
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Waddle was traded to the Denver Broncos, impacting the New England Patriots' strategy in acquiring a top wide receiver.
The Broncos traded a first-round pick, plus third and fourth-round picks in 2026, to obtain Waddle from the Miami Dolphins.
Denver's acquisition of Waddle may raise the trade cost for A.J. Brown, whom the Patriots are reportedly interested in.

title: 2 charged after car hits pedestrians outside of downtown Boston nightclub
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Darius Boyce, 45, and Maria Moraes, 25, charged after Jeep struck three pedestrians outside ICON nightclub in Boston.
Boyce faces felony assault charges with a weapon; Moraes charged with interfering with police and disorderly conduct.
Incident occurred on Feb. 28; victims recorded Jeep's license plate, leading to arrest and vehicle search.

title: Former President Biden makes surprise St. Patrick’s Day appearance in Boston
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former President Joe Biden made an unscheduled appearance at the Irish American Partnership's St. Patrick’s Day Breakfast in Boston.
Biden discussed his Irish ancestry and addressed current immigration challenges compared to the past during his speech.
Governor Maura Healey also highlighted Boston's Irish heritage and emphasized immigrants' contributions to American society.

title: 2 Boston College students injured in deck collapse during off-campus party
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Boston College students were injured in a deck collapse during an off-campus party in Brighton.
The deck railing broke, causing the students to fall from the second floor onto concrete, Boston police reported.
Police noted rotting wood and corroded nails on the deck, potentially masked by paint, and are investigating further.

title: Patriots rookie earns hefty performance bonus after strong 2025 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots rookie Craig Woodson played 950 defensive snaps in 2025, leading the team in total snaps.
Woodson earned an additional $1,149,910 from NFL performance payouts, alongside his $840,000 base salary.
In the 2025 postseason, Woodson recorded 30 tackles, four tackles for loss, and played prominently in Super Bowl LX.
